California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
July 30, 1991
Joe camic i a
City Council Member
City of Alameda
2263 Santa Clara Avenue, #315
A1aneda, CA 94501
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. : A-91-341
Dear Mr. Carnicia:
You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions
of the Politica1 Reform Act ("the na";tt1.r/ 
This letter vrill
address the questions raised in your letter of JuIy 3, 1991 and in
our telephone conversation on JuIy 12, 1991.
OUESTIONS
1. 
What are your reporting obligations if you change your
committee name from "Committee to EIect Joe Camicia" to ,'Friends
of Joe Camicia? "
2. 
When are you required to discl-ose activities r:elated to
your June 2O, L99]- fundrasier?
CONCLUSIONS
1. 
You must amend the cornrnittee,s Statenent of Organization
(Form 410) within l0 days of changing your committee nane.
2. 
You hust report the fundraiser activities 
on your 1991
semi-annual canpaign statement (Form 490) due JuIy 31, 1991.
l/ 
covernnent code Sections Srooo-9Lo15. AII statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise i.ndicated.
Cornmission regul:tions appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. AII- references to regulalions are to Title
2, Divisiorr 6 ol the California Code of Regulations.

Can icia, .Ioe
Page ?-
FACTS
You \^rere recently elected as a Councilmember for the City of
Santa Clara. During the campaign you established a committee
ca]Ied Icommittee to Elect Joe Canicia. " you now wish to change
the nane of that committee to rrFriends of Joe Camicia.,, you
recently filed a new candidate bank account form (Forn 502) under
the name of "Friends of Joe Camicia,, for officeholder expenses.
During our conversation on Jul,y 12, 1991, you indicated that you
no longer wanted to open a new campaign bank account but instead
wanted to use your bank account established for seeking office for
officeholder expenses. After your election, your committee held a
fund-raiser on June 20, ]-99L.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires candidates that receive or spend 91,Ooo or
more in a calendar year to fiLe a Statement of Orglnization (Form
41O) . (Section 84102.) whenever there is a change in any of the
information contained in a Statenent of Organization. suth as a
change of a committee,s nane. an amended Form 4lO nust be filed
within I0 days to reflect the change. (Section 84103.) In
addition, the Act requires candidates to establish one campaign
bank account for receiving contributions and making expenditures
associated with seekj.ng and holdj-ng an of f j-ce. (Seatio; 85201.)
Therefore, under the Act, you may use the bank account established
for your eLection to city council for officehoLder expenses. you
indicated during our telephone conversation that you nor+ wish to
terminate the nev, bank account. A Form 502 should be filed with
our office to indicate termi-nation of the account.
In addition, the Act requires candidate con.irolled committees
to file semi-annual campaign statements. (Section A12OO. ) AII
contributions received and expenditures made in connection with a
fundraiser held on June 20, 1991, are required to be disclosed. on
the semi-annual statement due JuIy 31. 1991. The statement must
cover the period beginning the day after the closing date of the
last campaign statenent filed through June 30, 1991. If no
previous statement has been fiIed, the period beEins January 1,
1991. (Section 82046. )


Encl"osed are Forms 410 and 5O2. $/hich you should use to amend
the current Statement of Organization, and to terninate the
campaign bank account. please do not hesitate to contact me at
(9L6) 322-5662 rf you have additional questions.
Camici a, ., oc
lrage l
Enclosures
Sincerely,
scott Hal labrin
AcE it gJ GeneralcounseI
